today_summary: GCM26 last printed $4,051.90 (most recent scrape, 13 DTE), essentially flat over the past 5 sessions (-$30.60, -0.7%) after round-tripping through a 5-day low of $4,005.80 on 7/14. Max pain sits $698 above spot at $4,750, exerting a gentle upward pull into expiry. Put-call OI ratio is 0.56 (137,984 calls vs 77,262 puts) — call-heavy chain even with spot chopping near the lower half of its recent range. Near-term OI walls cluster at $4,000 support (4,275 put OI) and $4,700 resistance (4,917 call OI), bracketing a $3,997-$4,107 1-sigma daily band on 21.6% ATM IV.
five_day_thesis: Over the last 5 sessions max pain drifted from $4,770 to $4,750 (-$20) in a single step, then held flat for four straight sessions — a stable magnet rather than a moving target. Spot was the volatile leg: it ran from $4,082.50 up to $4,113.60, then air-pocketed to $4,005.80 on 7/14 (-$107.80, -2.6% in one session) before recovering to $4,051.90, still $698 shy of max pain. GEX stayed net negative every single day this window (dealers persistently short gamma, flip strike parked $3,225-$3,295 — far below spot), and the negative-GEX intensity roughly doubled on the 7/14 selloff (-102.5 vs a -14 to -51 range on calmer days), confirming dealers amplified the drop rather than dampening it. ATM IV spiked 20.47% to 23.94% on 7/15 in the selloff's aftermath before compressing back to 21.6% today, while the $4,000 put wall and $5,370 call wall have not moved a single dollar in 5 sessions — the structural range is locked in even as spot whipsaws inside it.
what_changed: Max pain drifted $4,770 to $4,750 (-$20) then held flat for 4 straight sessions
GEX intensity spiked to -102.5 on the 7/14 selloff day versus a -14 to -51 range on calmer sessions — dealers amplified the drop
ATM IV spiked 20.47% to 23.94% (7/15) then compressed back to 21.6% today — vol premium round-tripped with spot
Put wall ($4,000) and call wall ($5,370) unchanged across all 5 sessions — structural range locked in
P/C OI ratio essentially flat, 0.57 to 0.56 — no meaningful directional OI shift
prediction: Bias is neutral to cautiously bullish while spot holds above the $4,000 put wall — the negative-GEX regime (flip strike near $3,250, far below spot) means a break under $4,000 risks a dealer-amplified accelerant move, echoing the -$107.80 single-day drop on 7/14. Above $4,000, expect grinding chop toward max pain $4,750, with resistance building first at $4,700 (near-term call wall, 4,917 OI) ahead of the $5,370 structural ceiling. Invalidation: a daily close below $4,000 flips the setup bearish and opens a retest of the mid-$3,900s.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot $4,051.90 sits mid-range between the $4,000 put wall and $4,700 near-term call wall; drift favors a grind toward max pain $4,750 while dealers stay short gamma.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips into the $4,000 put-OI floor (4,275 contracts) with confirmation — the most-defended level in the current chain.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above opens a path to $4,300 then the $4,700 call wall. Rejection here keeps price capped in the $4,000-$4,100 chop zone.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -1.6% below the entry zone. A close under $4,000 hands control to sellers inside the negative-GEX regime (flip strike near $3,250) — expect acceleration, not support.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 clears the mid-range OI shelf, take 50% off. T2 is the $4,700 near-term call wall (4,917 OI) — exit remaining ahead of the heavier $5,370 structural ceiling.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: GEX net negative all 5 sessions (today's ±$200-of-spot window: -102.4); IV 21.6% after a 23.94% spike on 7/15. Expect a 1.4%-2.6% daily range; size down 20-30% versus a positive-gamma tape.
